assertions.expectation: Checks for deprecated API usage that may have changed behavior in 1.30
assertions.reason: Response includes specific commands to check for deprecated API usage: 'kubectl get events --all-namespaces | grep -i deprecated' and 'kubectl get apiservices | grep False' with a dedicated section on deprecated API cleanup

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking admission webhooks — they may be rejecting or slowing down requests on the new version
assertions.reason: Response specifically mentions checking admission controllers with commands: 'kubectl get validatingwebhookconfigurations' and 'kubectl get mutatingwebhookconfigurations' and notes they could be causing bottlenecks

assertions.expectation: Advises checking if resource requests/limits need adjusting for any system components that changed
assertions.reason: Response includes commands to check system pod resource requests/limits and specifically mentions that Kubernetes 1.32 may have higher resource requirements, with example patch command for API server resources

assertions.expectation: Suggests examining kube-system pods for any that are crashlooping or restarting
assertions.reason: Response includes multiple commands to check kube-system pods status: 'kubectl get pods -n kube-system | grep apiserver', 'kubectl get pods -n kube-system | grep etcd', and 'kubectl get pods -n kube-system | grep coredns'

assertions.expectation: Includes commands to check API server metrics and latency (kubectl get --raw /metrics)
assertions.reason: Response includes the exact command 'kubectl get --raw /metrics | grep apiserver_request_duration' in the monitoring setup section

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking if NetworkPolicy or service mesh components need updating for the new version
assertions.reason: The response does not mention NetworkPolicy or service mesh components at all

assertions.expectation: Advises reviewing pod events for scheduling delays or readiness probe failures
assertions.reason: While the response mentions checking events for deprecated APIs, it does not specifically advise checking for scheduling delays or readiness probe failures

assertions.expectation: Mentions checking if HPA or VPA are behaving differently on the new version
assertions.reason: The response does not mention HPA (Horizontal Pod Autoscaler) or VPA (Vertical Pod Autoscaler) at all
